Comment: I definitely wouldnt take this class again if I could go back. It wasnt the worst thing in the world but as the semester progresses he gets more irritated with you if you ask questions. Kind of intimidating to ask a question because you dont want to get yelled at. if you study youll be fine. go to class and be on his good side
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Respected, Lecture heavy

Comment: Professor Nguyen loves coding and that is clear from the beginning. He loves to share this with all his students and he truly loves teaching. However, he can get very upset very quickly. High expectations but he also works hard to help you reach those expectations. I recommend to take him as early in the day as you can
Tags: Gives good feedback, Caring, Online Savvy

Comment:
Gives lots of extra credit on exam. Makes lighthearted jokes and tries to inspire everyone a lot. Pay a lot of attention to detail (sometimes too much though, especially about punctuality). High energy, slightly loud, may cut you off sometimes. As long as you don't take it personal it should be fine.

Has a bit of an accent but not too bad.
Tags: EXTRA CREDIT, Hilarious, Caring

Comment: Actually worst professor in my entire UM career. Had him at 8am on Mondays and it would ruin my week. He gets increasingly frustrated as class goes on, and refuses to accept that you don't understand coding. Called us dumb many times outright, would clap/snap at us for our attention, and doesn't like when you are even one line ahead or behind.

Actually my favorite professor at UM, all he cares about is being fair. If you come to class, try to participate and do your work (which is not complicated just may take a little bit of time) you will get a good grade. A lot of students in my class would agree this class is useful for CS majors. Class sounds scary but he makes the class fun.
Tags: Group projects, Amazing lectures, Hilarious

Comment: One of the best classes I have taken at UM. Very fun class, a lot of real-world examples, easy quizzes.
Tags: Group projects, Amazing lectures, Hilarious

Comment: As many other reviews have professed, Professor Nguyen tends to get a bad rep for the way he tries to help with messy code [usually in a stricter fashion]. However, he meant well and trying to help students better their theory and syntax. Honestly, if you get to know him a little better, he's just a chill dude who likes to program a lil'.
Tags: Participation matters

Comment: Gets a bad rep but is honestly a super caring and dedicated professor! Tends to yell or raise his voice during lab but don't take it personally. If you take anything from this it's to go to his student hours because he is really helpful will be more likely to help you in the overall course if you show that you care about his class.
Tags: Get ready to read, Clear grading criteria, Lots of homework

Comment: Hien is a super nice guy. I think a lot of people get intimidated by him in lab because he does tend to raise his voice at students but it's rarely meant in a mean or rude way he is just very passionate about what he teaches. If you care about coding and develop a connection with him, he is a great professor who cares and wants to see you succeed.
Tags: Participation matters, Caring

Comment: Super caring professor. Learned our names and made an effort to make sure you got the grade you wanted. Would take again and I'm grateful I had him my first semester!
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Caring

Comment: You can tell professor Nguyen is passionate about the subject he teaches. His course wasn't difficult, but labs were important to understanding the overall concepts. He genuinely just wants his students to succeed and his reviews don't do him justice. There is a cultural difference, but he is a kind professor who cares about his students.
Tags: Caring, Accessible outside class

Comment: I enjoyed taking his class. Although you may not understand him at first, Professor Nguyen does a good job at explaining everything in great detail and will make sure that you understand (RADAR.) Focus in the labs!!! That is where you will truly learn since the lectures are honestly not the most informative. Code his way or you will face penalties.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Caring, Accessible outside class

Comment: Professor Nguyen is a very kind and caring guy. He is always available to help you and wants to see you succeed. He wants you to have a strong foundation for computer science and works hard to make sure everyone understands. His labs are extremely helpful and he is a very passionate teacher. I really enjoyed taking this class.
Tags: Caring, Lecture heavy, Accessible outside class

Comment: I thoroughly enjoyed taking this class, Professor Nguyen is eager to see his students succeed and will go to great lengths to ensure this. He is very passionate about what he teaches, and it's part of what helped me succeed in the class. I would highly recommend taking him for this class!
Tags: Hilarious, Caring, Accessible outside class

Comment: All I have to say is never ask him a question. He is beyond rude and it is not a language barrier. Stick up for yourself if he becomes too outwardly rude, the way he embarrasses students is mind boggling. Listen to what he has to say ab the labs, ask the TA for help if you need, and you'll be fine!
Tags: Lecture heavy

Comment: He yaps and yells whenever you ask questions If you implement something advanced, he rejects it, even if it works. I knew everything he taught but didn't have credits (self-learned in HS) My school didn't have a credit system (international) Worst class experience ever. He wants the code his way and criticizes you for going beyond what he taught
Tags: Get ready to read, Hilarious

Comment: Pretty standard and easy intro coding course. Clearly he must've read all of the negative reviews online because if he ever raises his voice these days he tries to explain that he isn't screaming and it's just a cultural difference. Overall, a great guy who hasn't yelled at any of us yet. I feel bad since his reviews do not reflect him as a person.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Hilarious, Lecture heavy

Comment: Honestly do not understand why he has such low rating. He cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He makes lots of jokes and tries to get everyone involved. People who say that he yells probably do not understand the cultural difference. I was concerned he would be yelling at me too, but he is a sweet man who wants you to succeed.
Tags: Participation matters, Inspirational, Hilarious

Comment: I love this dude, the ratings don't give him enough credit. Maybe he has changed, but as i've read, I was concerned he would be a tough professor who is hard on his students. He actually connected with us, and gave us enough work to properly learn the material and do well for the exam.
Tags: Amazing lectures, Inspirational, Hilarious

Comment: For an entry level coding course, it neverrrrr had to be that complicated. Do NOT ask questions during labs unless you want to feel embarrassed or ashamed infront of your peers. He would yell and call students code ugly. I can tell he is a genuine person and wants students to learn, but his delivery is poor. WOA: MAKE FRIENDS !!!
Tags: Lots of homework, Graded by few things, Accessible outside class

Comment: Worst teacher I've ever had. Humiliates everyone and screams in the lab. You also can't understand one word he says. All he does is waste your time, as office hours are composed of him just yelling at you for asking any questions. Save yourself, and do NOT take his class.

Prof. Hien was all in all a great professor. Although at times he might be tough, if you take advantage of his office hours it isn't hard to do well in his class. Just be prepared to get shouted at if you code a different way than he does.
Tags: Tough grader, Clear grading criteria, Lots of homework

Comment: If you want to succeed in computer science I highly recommend not taking professor Hien. Hien has got some serious anger management issues, he often comes to class in a bad mood and proceeds to take it out on the students by shouting slamming desks and chairs. He continuously puts down students making his classroom a hostile work environment.
Tags: Tough grader, Participation matters, Clear grading criteria

Comment: He is extremely helpful if you are struggling dont get me wrong. However, he yells at his students when they make the slightest mistake. Also, everything needs to be done his way, if not even if its right but not his way he will mark it wrong. The final project is also super hard. I honestly would not recommend taking this class unless you have to.
Tags: Tough grader, Lots of homework, Test heavy

Comment: He was caring and willing to help you. If you are struggling, go to the office hours. IT IS VERY HELPFUL. The only thing I disliked was that he yelled during the labs. He can be reactive when you don't follow his instructions. It isn't out of ill intention, just a little rough. Going to the labs did give me anxiety tho but overall, I enjoyed him.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Caring, Accessible outside class

Comment: He comes off as sweet and tried to make lots of jokes but he takes a lot of time overexplaing. He also gets very upset if you make a mistake in your code and will probably yell at you, but to me it just seemed like a cultural difference and just wants you to understand. The class isn't unbearable because of him but don't step on his toes.

He was my lab professor for CSC 120. He wants things to be done exactly the same as he does and any small deviation would lead to him shouting at you. When you don't understand something he would repeat the same things until eventually just giving you the answer. He has a heavy accent and that also made it very difficult to understand him at times.

He was actually my lab professor for CSC120, the guy would constantly start shouting at kids for making the slightest changes to his code. He just shouts all lab long and gaslights you into thinking this is making you a real programmer. You'd ask him for help and he was often wrong, and when you told him that he'd shout at you and then walk away.

I personally love Professor Nguyen. However, those who are not tech-savvy should beware. Professor Nguyen expects you to be caught up on readings and be able to maintain his speed. He comes off aggressive but it is never intentional and he always tries to help. If you want to succeed in this class do all the readings and attend office hours often.
Tags: Get ready to read, Accessible outside class

Comment: He makes the entry level cs class unnecessarily complicated. Labs are disasters, instructions are always not clear. He will teach the class with mood because the previous section's people didn't do the task well. Disseminating negative energy and lots of yelling. take him carefully if you don't know anything about programming.

What a great professor. His priority is your learning! He will find any way possible to help you understand the lesson we're going over, he makes the lectures FUN, he has a very interactive teaching style. He sends out polls for you to ask him questions so he can answer them in class, in case you're nervous of asking in a room full of people.
Tags: Participation matters, Hilarious, Caring

Comment:
Terrible professor. Extremely easy class. If you already have knowledge in programming, you will not get much out of this class. It is very difficult to understand him and he made grammatical errors on his exams (LOL). 

His lectures are useless too and are full of rants and digressions.

Professer Hien is new to the University of Miami and I really enjoyed taking his course. He is extremely caring towards students and goes above and beyond to help them outside of class. Can be a little aggressive while explaining difficult concepts if youre a newbie but dont take it personally. He is always willing to help and wants you to learn
Tags: Participation matters, Caring, Accessible outside class

Comment: Dr. Nguyen cares for his students and will answer any questions you have. This class was enjoyable. There are weekly assignments, weekly lab homework, and a midterm and final project. If you enjoy the material being taught, you will have no problem getting a high grade in this class; if you do not "enjoy" the material, it is still fun.
Tags: Get ready to read, Lots of homework, Caring

Comment: It was a great class for an introduction to computer programming. He was always available to help with questions. If you do the homework/labs you will do fine. Go to the labs if you need help. Great class, definitely take him!
Tags: Gives good feedback, Caring, Accessible outside class

Comment: Very nice guy, kinda hard to understand. Easy class if you do the homework, and if you do fine on the labs you don't really need to go to class.
Tags: Lots of homework, Hilarious, Accessible outside class
